title: Data Analyst - Marketing Strategy & Intelligence
required_skills: Quantitative degree (Statistics, Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science)
Data storytelling – translating complex findings into actionable insights
bonus_skills: Experience in travel, e-commerce, or digital marketing industry
A/B testing, geo testing, or causal inference
BigQuery or other big data platforms
matched_skills: BI / dashboarding tools – Power BI (PL-300 certified) and Google Looker Studio
Marketing metrics, KPI dashboards & ROI reporting
Data storytelling and executive-ready insight communication
Strong business acumen across e-commerce, FinTech, and IoT sectors
E-commerce and digital marketing industry experience (Moli Media)
Campaign performance analysis and post-event ROI evaluation
missing_skills: SQL – not mentioned anywhere in CV
BigQuery or big data platform experience
A/B testing / geo testing / causal inference
5+ years dedicated Data Analyst role (CV background is product marketing)
Quantitative degree (CV has Public Relations + Management degrees)
core_responsibilities: Translate complex marketing data into actionable insights that shape global marketing strategy and investment decisions
Partner with marketing stakeholders to define KPIs and measure campaign success across all channels
Build dashboards and reports that surface trends and tell data-driven stories for technical and non-technical audiences
Conduct deep-dive analyses to understand performance drivers, detect anomalies, and recommend strategic actions
Ensure marketing data quality and consistency across multiple data sources
recommended_emphasis: Highlight Power BI certification (PL-300) and Looker Studio experience prominently — these partially cover the Looker/BI tooling requirement
Frame KPI dashboard and ROI reporting work at Hapag-Lloyd and Moli Media as data analyst deliverables, not just project coordination
Emphasize the VfB Stuttgart KPI impact measurement framework as evidence of structured analytical thinking
Showcase e-commerce and digital marketing analytics work at Moli Media (audience data, ROI analysis, post-campaign reporting)
Address the SQL gap directly in the cover letter — mention willingness to upskill or any informal SQL exposure
Leverage the quantitative reasoning demonstrated through financial variance reporting at HSBC to partially compensate for non-quantitative degree
